title: SACRAMENTO – Today the Legislature passed AB 1346, authored by Assemblymember Marc Berman (D-Menlo Park) and Lorena Gonzalez (D-San Diego), which will phase out the sale of new gas

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Small off-road engines emit smog-forming pollutants at levels projected to outpace those from passenger cars this year.",
    "Leaf blowers, lawn mowers, and other equipment with small gas-powered engines emit staggering levels of air pollution.",
    "Workers who breathe in exhaust from small gas-powered equipment face disproportionate health risks, including asthma, cardiovascular disease, and cancer.",
    "AB 1346 would require the California Air Resources Board (CARB) to set emissions requirements for small off-road engines to zero by 2024 or whenever CARB determines is feasible.",
    "Small off-road engines emit high levels of oxides of nitrogen (NOx), reactive organic gases (ROG), and particulate matter (PM), which adversely affect air quality and human health.",
    "By 2031, NOx and ROG emissions from small off-road engines are projected to be twice those from passenger cars in California.",
    "Small gas engines contribute to air pollution and can cause asthma and other health issues for workers who use them.",
    "California faces some of the most difficult air pollution challenges in the United States, and small off-road engines are a growing source of this health burden.",
    "AB 1346 will help achieve the emissions reductions needed to improve air quality and meet California’s climate goals."
  ]
}
```
